records.title: Coarse restricted-domain gating suppressing benign scientific inquiry
records.summary: A restricted-domain classification failure in which a frontier AI system refuses, pauses, reroutes, or degrades benign educational, contextual, analytical, or non-operational scientific requests because they fall within a broad field such as biology, biochemistry, chemistry, cybersecurity, or another dual-use domain, without first resolving the requested depth, operational applicability, optimisation potential, interaction trajectory, or harm profile. In Claude Fable 5, intentionally broad biology safeguards can capture ordinary scientific and medical questions and automatically route them to Claude Opus 4.8 or pause the conversation, reducing access to the selected model even where the request presents no meaningful operational escalation risk.

records.title: Weak-signal ambiguity collapse under recoverable image-generation classification
records.summary: A governance and UX-representation failure mode in which an AI system refuses a request and presents a user-facing rationale that appears disconnected from the visible prompt, insufficiently specific, or not useful for recovery. The observed case concerns an image-generation request that appeared benign on its face but produced a refusal attributed to harassment, discrimination, or bullying. The record does not assert that the underlying classifier was necessarily wrong; it records the user-facing failure where the system provides no meaningful clarification, remediation pathway, or explanation of whether the refusal arose from visible prompt content, earlier context, accumulated weak signals, rate or load constraints, inherited image state, or generic fallback routing. A follow-up audit further identifies image prompt custody / prompt transformation opacity as an additional contributing mechanism: the same observed pattern may occur where custody is lost between the user-authored prompt, system-transformed prompt, safety-normalized prompt, classifier-visible prompt, renderer-facing prompt, classifier outcome, and final user-facing explanation. Follow-up review indicates that the observed refusal pattern may arise not only from weak-signal ambiguity collapse, but also from loss of custody across image-prompt transformation and failure-classification collapse. A benign user-authored prompt may be transformed, safety-normalized, classified, blocked, or failed in a downstream renderer/tool pathway, while the final explanation is surfaced as if the user-authored prompt itself contained the prohibited category. This remains the same observed FM-0023 failure family, not a new FM-0024.

The repair adds explicit coverage for sovereign assurance boundary porosity and regulated runtime lane governance, treating FedRAMP as the evidenced example rather than the name or outer boundary of the doctrine. It implements a governance failure-taxonomy subtype for Sovereign Assurance Boundary Porosity Failure and a SECURITY boundary primitive for Sovereign Assurance Boundary, while preserving reverse leakage and authority bleed-through as risk vectors rather than confirmed harms unless separately evidenced.

records.title: Provisional Interaction and Pre-Invocation Tool-Arming Constraint
records.summary: This patch records the Caelestis corpus repair to CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 §7.4.1 responding to VIGIL-2026-FM-0001. The repair splits §7.4.1 into weak-trigger interpretation, provisional interaction / accidental submission handling, and pre-invocation tool-arming constraints. It clarifies that lexical mention of executable modalities, upload-without-instruction events, accidental submission, partial prompts, staged uploads, draft prompt construction, or incomplete multimodal assembly do not by themselves authorise tool invocation, tool arming, model routing, renderer preparation, modality-specific UI activation, or execution-path reservation.

records.title: Deception-adjacent classification and action-pathway attribution discipline
records.summary: This patch note records the Caelestis Architecture repair response to VIGIL-2026-FM-0025, VIGIL-2026-FM-0026, and VIGIL-2026-FM-0027. The repair adds Deception-Adjacent Classification Collapse to the CAM failure taxonomy and Action-Pathway, Attribution, and Audit-Surface Integrity to Annex L. The patch prevents deception, scheming, cheating, malice, self-governance, personal preference, or bad-faith labels from hardening before reviewers decompose objective, constraint, action, reporting, provenance, rights-bearing, narrative/persona, and attribution pathways.

records.title: Pause, checkpoint, and resumable execution controls for long-running AI agents
records.summary: Proposal to define a runtime governance requirement for long-running AI agents, coding agents, cloud tasks, and repository-connected workflows to support non-destructive pause, checkpoint, and resume controls. The proposal responds to the continuity risk that arises when a user must interrupt an agentic task because of ordinary real-world constraints such as aircraft landing, battery loss, network loss, device shutdown, meeting interruption, or platform timeout, but the interface offers only destructive stop or cancellation. The proposed control would require the system to preserve a temporary branch, stash, patch, state summary, task log, or equivalent checkpoint before suspension where safe and technically feasible.
